payload.text: Build a website for an iOS and MacOS app called Today (SwiftUI) native that treats each day as a fresh to-do list.  Core behavior of the app: - The app always opens to Today’s list (clean slate each calendar day). - Tasks not completed from previous days automatically move to a Drawer (overflow/backlog). - Users can pull tasks from the Drawer into Today (and optionally send tasks back to the Drawer). - Tasks can be marked done.  This will be a marketing site for the app. Make sure to advertise the clean aesthetics and minimalism of the design and the concept that every day starts fresh.

**Defining the Scope**

I'm currently focused on defining the core scope. It's clear the goal is a marketing website for the "Today" app, highlighting its iOS/macOS nature. The key feature highlights are SwiftUI, the daily to-do list refresh, and how tasks transition. I need to figure out what information is important to communicate.


**Refining the Aesthetic**

I'm now thinking about the target audience and overall vibe. It looks like it's productivity enthusiasts and minimalists. The design needs to be clean, whitespace-heavy with system fonts like San Francisco and a black/white/gray color palette with subtle accents. I'm focusing on typography and spacing. I'm also planning the key sections for the marketing site.


**Designing the Demo**

I'm now figuring out the best way to showcase the "Today vs. Drawer" functionality. I'm thinking of building an interactive demo right in the browser. It should be a mini-simulation of the app's core loop. I am brainstorming potential testimonial elements, a simple footer design, and the overall tech stack, leaning towards React and Tailwind.


**Integrating AI into Demo**

I've decided to integrate AI into the interactive demo. My plan is to include a "Need inspiration? Ask AI to plan your day" feature, using the Gemini API. This will dynamically generate tasks for the "Today" list based on user roles like "Developer" or "Student." This satisfies the expertise for the persona constraints and supports user needs.

I'm structuring the codebase for this functionality: index.html for CDN and root; index.tsx for entry; App.tsx for layout and routing; and the components: Hero, FeatureSection, InteractiveDemo (complex logic), and GeminiGenerator (AI component).


**Implementing the AI Feature**

I'm now implementing the AI feature using `geminiService.ts` to generate tasks for the interactive demo. I'm focusing on the state management for `todayTasks` and `drawerTasks`, and defining actions for task completion and movement. I've designed the specific prompt for Gemini to generate realistic to-do items as JSON, based on user roles, so I can display the results.

**Refining the UI Elements**

I'm refining the user interface elements and styling. My current color palette includes `bg-zinc-50`, `text-zinc-900`, and `blue-600` for primary actions. I'm using a sans-serif font with tracking-tight for headings. I'm also building a CSS-only iPhone frame for the hero image and app preview to create the illusion of an app UI.

**Mapping Implementation Steps**

I've mapped out the step-by-step implementation plan. I'll begin by setting up the necessary files: `metadata.json`, `index.html`, and `index.tsx`. I'll integrate `geminiService.ts` for task generation, and then develop the required components, starting with `Button` and `PhoneMockup`.


**Structuring the Application**

I'm now focusing on structuring the application's components. I'm prioritizing the `InteractiveDemo` component as the functional core, the `Hero` component for marketing copy and the phone mockup, and `App.tsx` to assemble these sections. I've re-prioritized based on the user request.

I've also begun to write the code for the main files. I've created the `metadata.json` and `index.html` files, integrating Tailwind CSS. I'm going to use `lucide-react` for icons directly, since they're standard here, for the best result, rather than SVG imports.


**Organizing Core Components**

I'm now focusing on arranging the file structure for the app. I am creating `App.tsx` for the landing page assembly, `InteractiveDemo.tsx` for the core logic, and `PhoneMockup.tsx` for the visual representation. I am also planning `geminiService.ts` for AI task generation. I am planning `index.tsx` as the app's entry, and assuming standard environment capability.

entries.description: Root HTML file with Tailwind CSS and Inter font
Service to interact with Google Gemini API for generating sample tasks
SVG Icons components to avoid external dependencies issues in some environments
A fully functional mini-app simulation to demonstrate the product's core loop
Main application layout component containing the landing page structure

import React from 'react'; import { InteractiveDemo } from './components/InteractiveDemo'; import { CheckIcon, ArrowRightIcon, ArchiveIcon, SunIcon, DownloadIcon } from './components/Icons'; function App() { return (
{/* Navigation */}
Today
How it works Manifesto Pricing
Get App
{/* Hero Section */}
Now available for iOS 18

Every day is a
fresh start.

The minimal to-do app that clears your list every morning. Focus on today, archive the rest for later. Built natively for Apple devices.

{/* Interactive Demo Component */}
{/* Feature Grid */}

Why Today is different

Most to-do apps become graveyards of guilt. Today is designed to forgive you and keep you moving forward.

The Morning Reset

When you wake up, your list is empty. No red badges, no overdue alerts. Just a clean slate to decide what matters today.

The Drawer

Unfinished tasks aren't deleted—they are safe in the Drawer. Drag them out when you have time, or leave them there until you do.

SwiftUI Native

Built with 100% native SwiftUI. It feels like part of your phone. Haptic feedback, smooth animations, and widget support included.

{/* Manifesto / Minimalist Section */}

"Productivity isn't about doing more things.
It's about doing the right things."

We built Today because we were tired of apps that felt like work. We wanted a tool that felt like a breath of fresh air.

{/* Footer */}
Today
© {new Date().getFullYear()} Today App Inc. All rights reserved.

); } export default App;

payload.text: Remove the section in the middle and replace with the screenshot I provided. Remove pricing nav bar. Also finish the Manifesto page.

**Eliminating Interactive Demo**

I'm removing the "Interactive Demo" section from the "Today" app website. It's being replaced with a screenshot as a more concise and direct representation.


**Recreating Screenshot UI**

I'm now focused on recreating the provided screenshot's UI using HTML/CSS. I've begun to analyze the screenshot, breaking it down into its constituent elements. I'm noting the dark mode MacOS window style, the sidebar's structure with its active "Today" menu item, and the main content area with its header, title, stats, and filter pills. I plan to use Tailwind to achieve a pixel-perfect (or as close as possible) visual representation.


**Prioritizing Tasks for Today**

I'm now prioritizing the current tasks. First up, I must finish the statistics section of my thesis. Then, I need to email my professors back about the thesis review date. After that, I will attend to the Telus SIM issue and finally complete the PlotNeuralNet diagrams. There's also the "Add Task" floating button to consider. Additionally, I need to remove the "Pricing" link from the navigation bar.
I also need to finish the manifesto page, expanding its current content to include a full section. I have a lot to attend to.


**Implementing App Mockup**

I'm now implementing the `AppMockup` component. I will start by replicating the dark MacOS window and will include the sidebar with window controls and menu items. Then, I'll build the main content area, including the header, date, task stats, filter tags, and task list items with their specific styles and the add button. I'll use Tailwind's colors to match the dark theme, specifically slate, zinc, and gray. I'll also add the necessary icons for the mockup, such as sidebar icon, history, settings, calendar, and bell icons.


**Verifying App Implementation**

I'm now integrating the `AppMockup` component into `App.tsx`. I've removed the `InteractiveDemo` import and replaced its usage in the Hero section with `<AppMockup />`. I also eliminated the "Pricing" link from the navbar and am rewriting the Manifesto section to elaborate on "Graveyard of guilt" and "The Drawer." The philosophy will be centered around starting fresh, forgiving oneself, and focusing on the present. I'm focusing on dark-mode styling for the mockup, specifically colors and rounded corners.

To fulfill your request, I will make the following changes:

1.  **Create a new `AppMockup.tsx` component**: This will recreate the specific MacOS dark-mode interface shown in your screenshot using Tailwind CSS, replacing the previous interactive demo.
2.  **Update `Icons.tsx`**: Add necessary icons (Sidebar, History, Settings, Calendar, Bell) for the new mockup.
3.  **Update `App.tsx`**:
    *   Replace the `InteractiveDemo` with the new `AppMockup`.
    *   Remove the "Pricing" link from the navigation bar.
    *   Expand the "Manifesto" section into a complete "Design Philosophy" section with detailed copy about the "Fresh Start" concept.

Here are the changes:

entries.description: Added icons for Sidebar, History, Settings, Calendar, and Bell to support the new App Mockup.
New component recreating the dark mode MacOS interface screenshot.

import React from 'react'; import { AppMockup } from './components/AppMockup'; import { CheckIcon, ArrowRightIcon, ArchiveIcon, SunIcon, DownloadIcon } from './components/Icons'; function App() { return (
{/* Navigation */}
Today
How it works Manifesto
Get App
{/* Hero Section */}
Now available for iOS 18

Every day is a
fresh start.

The minimal to-do app that clears your list every morning. Focus on today, archive the rest for later. Built natively for Apple devices.

{/* Mockup Component */}
{/* Feature Grid */}

Why Today is different

Most to-do apps become graveyards of guilt. Today is designed to forgive you and keep you moving forward.

The Morning Reset

When you wake up, your list is empty. No red badges, no overdue alerts. Just a clean slate to decide what matters today.

The Drawer

Unfinished tasks aren't deleted—they are safe in the Drawer. Drag them out when you have time, or leave them there until you do.

SwiftUI Native

Built with 100% native SwiftUI. It feels like part of your phone. Haptic feedback, smooth animations, and widget support included.

{/* Manifesto / Design Philosophy Section */}

Our Philosophy

"Productivity isn't about doing more things. It's about doing the right things."

The Guilt Graveyard

Traditional to-do apps are designed to accumulate. You add a task, you miss the deadline, and it turns red. Over time, your "Today" view becomes a scrolling list of past failures. We call this the "Guilt Graveyard."

When you open your phone and see 43 overdue tasks, you don't feel motivated. You feel overwhelmed. So you close the app and do nothing.

The Fresh Start

We believe that every sunrise is a pardon. Yesterday's failures shouldn't haunt today's potential.

That's why Today wipes your slate clean every single morning. Tasks you didn't finish aren't deleted—they are gently moved to "The Drawer," a safe backlog that doesn't clutter your daily focus. You can retrieve them when you're ready, but they won't stare at you until you are.

Ready to forgive yourself?

Join thousands of people who have swapped anxiety for clarity.

{/* Footer */}
Today
